title: tetraclea coulteri var. subinclusa
intro: Tetraclea coulteri var. subinclusa, also known as Coulter's woodland honeysuckle or subinclusa woodland honeysuckle, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Tetraclea coulteri plant, a flowering plant native to California in the United States. The medicinal properties of Tetraclea coulteri var. subinclusa include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in traditional herbal medicine. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
